policy_statement: Hong Kong will strive to achieve carbon neutrality before 2050 and set more proactive strategies and measures to reduce carbon emissions.
verbatim_text:
The Chief Executive announced in the 2020 Policy Address that Hong Kong would strive to achieve carbon neutrality before 2050.

The Hong Kong Special Administrative Region will strive to achieve carbon neutrality before  2050.  To  this  end,  the  Government will  set  out  more  proactive  strategies  and measures to reduce carbon emissions.
extraction_rationale: Explicit government commitment with a clear deadline (before 2050) and stated intent to implement strategies/measures to reduce emissions.
reasoning: The verbatim text contains an economy-wide aspiration (“strive to achieve carbon neutrality”) with a timeframe (“before 2050”), but it does not specify any measurable interim or final quantified target definition, sectoral targets, or enforceable mechanism. The language is weak/aspirational (“strive”) and the “strategies and measures” are not described as binding requirements. Under the strict rule for individual policies, missing a quantifiable target makes this NON-SOUND and caps confidence.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The text says Hong Kong would "strive to achieve carbon neutrality before 2050" but provides no measurable emissions pathway, % reductions, caps, or interim milestones; 'carbon neutrality' is stated as an aim without quantified metrics in the excerpt. Timeline: Explicitly states "before 2050". Binding mechanism: No indication of an ordinance/mandate/regulation; it says the Government "will set out more proactive strategies and measures" which is not an enforceable requirement. Spatial specificity: Applies to "Hong Kong" / "The Hong Kong Special Administrative Region". Weak language: Uses aspirational wording "would strive" and "will strive". Strong language: No mandatory verbs with hard numbers are present. STRICT VALID RULE fails because has_quantifiable_target='No' (gold standard not met) and weak language is present without any binding mechanism.

verbatim_text: Adopting the prevailing technologies that are relatively mature and cost-effective, we aim to reduce the total carbon emissions by half before 2035 from the 2005 level, i.e. from about 40 million tonnes in 2005 to nearly 20 million tonnes in 2035.
extraction_rationale: Quantified economy-wide emissions target with timeframe and baseline.
reasoning: The statement includes a quantified, outcome-based emissions reduction target (halve total carbon emissions from ~40 Mt to ~20 Mt) with a clear baseline year (2005) and a deadline (“in 2035” / “before 2035”). Under the strict rule, it has the “gold standard” (timeline + quantifiable target) but fails the language-strength condition because weak language is present and there is no binding mechanism cited. Therefore it is NON-SOUND.

The Government plans to allocate about $240 billion in the next 15 to 20 years to implement mitigation and adaptation measures to combat climate change, including the four main actions mentioned in this Chapter, covering energy saving and green buildings, RE, green transport and waste management, etc.

Therefore it is not auditable/measurable enough under the strict individual-policy rule and should be classified as NON-SOUND.

Timeline: Yes. The text specifies a time horizon: "in the next 15 to 20 years."
